quiz.title: Module 4 Quiz: Machine Learning Concepts
quiz.description: Test your understanding of how AI learns. 8 questions, auto-graded.

questions.question: What is machine learning?

options.text: Writing every rule manually in code
Teaching computers to learn from examples instead of explicit rules
A robot that can walk and talk
A type of computer memory

questions.explanation: Machine learning is a type of AI where computers learn patterns from labeled examples rather than being given step-by-step rules.

questions.question: In supervised learning, what does 'labeled data' mean?

options.text: Data that has been sorted alphabetically
Data stored in a folder with a name tag
Examples that have been tagged with the correct answers
Data compressed into a ZIP file

questions.explanation: In supervised learning, labeled data means each example comes with its correct answer (label), like an image tagged 'cat' or 'dog'.

questions.question: Which type of machine learning uses rewards and penalties to improve over time?

options.text: Supervised learning
Unsupervised learning
Reinforcement learning
Transfer learning

questions.explanation: Reinforcement learning trains an agent through rewards and penalties — like training a puppy or teaching AI to play games.

questions.question: What is an 'epoch' in machine learning training?

options.text: A single test example shown to the model
One complete pass through the entire training dataset
The final accuracy score of a model
A type of neural network layer

questions.explanation: An epoch is one complete pass through all the training data. Models typically train for many epochs to improve accuracy.

questions.question: What is 'overfitting' in machine learning?

options.text: When a model performs well on new, unseen data
When a model is too simple to learn any patterns
When a model memorizes training data but fails on new examples
When training takes too long

questions.explanation: Overfitting is when a model learns the training data too specifically (like memorizing answers) and can't generalize to new examples.

questions.question: Why do we use a separate 'test set' when evaluating a model?

options.text: To train the model faster
To measure how well the model works on data it has never seen before
To label new data more efficiently
To reduce the size of the training data

questions.explanation: A test set contains examples the model hasn't seen during training. This gives an honest measure of real-world performance.

questions.question: In the four stages of supervised learning, what is the correct order?

options.text: Test → Train → Label → Collect
Collect → Label → Train → Test
Label → Collect → Test → Train
Train → Label → Collect → Test

questions.explanation: The correct pipeline is: (1) Collect data, (2) Label data, (3) Train the model, (4) Test the model on unseen examples.

questions.question: What does a neural network's 'hidden layer' do?

options.text: Stores the raw input data
Outputs the final prediction
Detects increasingly complex patterns from the input
Downloads more training data from the internet

questions.explanation: Hidden layers in a neural network detect progressively complex features — early layers find simple patterns (edges), later layers find complex ones (shapes, objects).
